title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Four: Which concerns marriage and prophecy (Fragment 4)
text:
“Get it out! Read it!” said Fatima in even greater excitement.

Abdullah could not see what the fuss was about, but he unfolded the paper. It had a few lines of writing on it, brown and faded and definitely his father’s. He turned toward the hanging lamp with it. Now that Hakim had shut the main doors, the general purpleness of the emporium made it hard to see in there.

“He can barely see!” said Fatima.

Assif said, “No wonder. There’s no light in here. Bring him into the room at the back. The overhead shutters are open there.”

He and Hakim took hold of Abdullah’s shoulders and pushed and hustled him toward the back of the shop. Abdullah was so busy trying to read the pale and scribbly writing of his father that he let them push him until he was positioned under the big overhead louvers in the living room behind the emporium. That was better. Now he knew why his father had been so disappointed in him. The writing said:

These are the words of the wise fortune-teller: “This son of yours will not follow you in your trade. Two years after your death, while he is still a very young man, he will be raised above all others in this land. As Fate decrees it, so I have spoken.”

My son’s fortune is a great disappointment to me. Let Fate send me other sons to follow in my trade, or I have wasted forty gold pieces on this prophecy.

“As you see, a great future awaits you, dear boy,” said Assif.

Somebody giggled.

Abdullah looked up from the paper, a little bemused. There seemed to be a lot of scent in the air.

The giggle came again, two of it, from in front of him.

Abdullah’s eyes snapped forward. He felt them bulge. Two extremely fat young women stood in front of him. They met his bulging eyes and giggled again, coyly. Both were dressed to kill in shiny satin and ballooning gauze—pink on the right, yellow on the left one—and hung with more necklaces and bracelets than seemed probable. In addition, the pink one, who was fattest, had a pearl dangling on her forehead, just below her carefully frizzed hair. The yellow one, who was only just not fattest, wore a sort of amber tiara and had even frizzier hair. Both wore a very large amount of makeup, which was, in both cases, a severe error.

As soon as they were sure Abdullah’s attention was on them—and it was; he was riveted with horror—each girl drew a veil from behind her ample shoulders—a pink veil on the left and a yellow on the right—and draped it chastely across her head and face. “Greetings, dear husband!” they chorused from beneath the veils.

“ What !” exclaimed Abdullah.

“We veil ourselves,” said the pink one.

“Because you should not look at our faces,” said the yellow one.

“Until we are married,” finished the pink.

“There must be some mistake!” said Abdullah.

“Not in the least,” said Fatima. “These are my niece’s two nieces who are here to marry you. Didn’t you hear me say I was going to look out for a couple of wives for you?”

The two nieces giggled again. “He’s ever so handsome,” said the yellow one.

After a fairly long pause, in which he swallowed hard and did his best to control his feelings, Abdullah said politely, “Tell me, O relatives of my father’s first wife, have you known of the prophecy which was made at my birth for a long time?”

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Four: Which concerns marriage and prophecy

vocabulary.definition: Enough or more than enough; plentiful.
vocabulary.example: There was ample space for the guests.

vocabulary.definition: To be in store for; to be waiting for someone.
vocabulary.example: An exciting adventure awaits you.

vocabulary.definition: Puzzled, confused, or bewildered.
vocabulary.example: She had a bemused expression on her face.

vocabulary.definition: Ornaments worn around the wrist.
vocabulary.example: She wore gold bracelets on her wrist.

vocabulary.definition: To swell or protrude outward.
vocabulary.example: The bag bulged with things inside.

vocabulary.definition: In a modest or pure manner, especially in dress or behaviour.
vocabulary.example: The bride was chastely dressed.

vocabulary.definition: To say or shout something simultaneously with others.
vocabulary.example: The children chorused 'thank you' to the teacher.

vocabulary.definition: In a shy or reserved manner intended to be alluring or flirtatious.
vocabulary.example: She smiled coyly at him.

vocabulary.definition: The feeling of sadness or displeasure caused by the non-fulfilment of one's hopes or expectations.
vocabulary.example: She felt deep disappointment when she failed the exam.

vocabulary.definition: A large retail store selling a wide variety of goods.
vocabulary.example: He bought his tools at the emporium downtown.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of great enthusiasm and eagerness.
vocabulary.example: Fatima expressed her excitement loudly.

vocabulary.definition: A person who claims to predict the future.
vocabulary.example: The fortune-teller predicted good luck.

vocabulary.definition: Having hair styled in small tight curls or waves.
vocabulary.example: She had frizzed hair after the humidity.

vocabulary.definition: A display of unnecessary or excessive concern or activity.
vocabulary.example: They made a big fuss over a small mistake.

vocabulary.definition: To laugh lightly in a nervous, affected, or silly manner.
vocabulary.example: She giggled at the funny joke.

vocabulary.definition: To push or shove roughly or quickly; to hurry someone along.
vocabulary.example: They hustled the crowd out of the stadium.

vocabulary.definition: Cosmetics such as lipstick or powder applied to the face.
vocabulary.example: She bought new makeup before the party.

vocabulary.definition: Having a husband or wife; joined in marriage.
vocabulary.example: They have been happily married for ten years.

vocabulary.definition: A daughter of one's brother or sister, or of the brother or sister of one's spouse.
vocabulary.example: My niece is coming to visit us.

vocabulary.definition: Situated or used above the level of the head.
vocabulary.example: The plane flew overhead.

vocabulary.definition: To place someone or something in a particular spot or way.
vocabulary.example: The photographer positioned the model carefully.

vocabulary.definition: A prediction of the future made under divine inspiration.
vocabulary.example: The ancient prophecy foretold the king's downfall.

vocabulary.definition: Having the colour of purple, a colour intermediate between red and blue.
vocabulary.example: The room was decorated in shades of purple.

vocabulary.definition: Completely engrossed or absorbed; fixed attentively.
vocabulary.example: The audience was riveted by the performance.

vocabulary.definition: A distinctive smell, especially one that is pleasant.
vocabulary.example: The scent of roses filled the garden.

vocabulary.definition: A metal or wooden cover for a window or door to provide protection or privacy.
vocabulary.example: She closed the shutters to block out the sun.

vocabulary.definition: A jeweled ornamental crown worn by women on formal occasions.
vocabulary.example: The princess wore a sparkling tiara.

vocabulary.definition: To open or spread out from a folded position.
vocabulary.example: She unfolded the map to see where they were.

vocabulary.definition: A piece of fine material worn to protect or conceal the face.
vocabulary.example: The bride wore a delicate veil.
